seo.title: Drowned Coffee Ice Cream - Marinda Kook

html:
(This is one of the Italians' favourite desserts: Affogato. The word means 'drowned'. The idea is that you 'drown' really good vanilla ice cream with espresso. For Anna, we decided she loves coffee so much that we'd give her a double Affogato … with coffee ice cream!)

Ingredients

  • 175 g condensed milk (half a tin)
  • 30 ml instant espresso powder
  • 30 ml coffee liqueur (such as Tia Maria)
  • 300 ml cream, stiffly whipped
  • real espresso or good coffee

Method

Mix the condensed milk, coffee powder and liqueur thoroughly together. Fold the mixture into the cream.

Spoon the mixture into an airtight plastic container, seal well and freeze overnight or for at least six hours.

To serve, make each guest a small cup of espresso (or very strong black coffee) with no sugar.

Scoop a generous portion of ice cream into a bowl and serve with the espresso on the side.

Each guest then pours the hot espresso over the ice cream and tucks in!

Makes 4 – 6 servings.

Easy!
